pending: **Decision needed**: bump the synthetic-juror gap threshold from 10s → 30s as a quick follow-up, OR defer to the dispatch-ownership redesign (`prop-20260517-172942-05`). Real-flow gap measured at 11s during this session's verification — current threshold is one second too lenient to flag a fast-typing fabricator.

**`prop-20260517-172942-05`** (large effort, filed) — invert juror dispatch ownership. Have `atone.sh` internally run `claude -p --persona=juror < case.json > verdict.json` so the agent is removed from verdict-composition entirely. Open sub-questions in the proposal body: `claude -p` reliability + JSON parseability, timeout fallback (`juror_unavailable:true`?), case-file lifecycle, whether to keep the manual `atone juror` subcommand for user-driven recording.

Smoke-test row `mist-20260517-174004-ea` remains in the production log per user's choice — no cleanup pending, but worth a note in any future audit script that this row was an unintended verification artifact, not a real mistake event.
